title: How to Prepare for an FDA Inspection from the UK
author: B. Subramanian
excerpt: FDA inspection preparation for UK and EU sites: map 21 CFR 211 to EU GMP, ready your site, rehearse the team and respond to a Form 483 with confidence.
cover_image_prompt: A photorealistic clinical-luxury pharmaceutical banner in a cool teal and cyan palette over deep navy, with a subtle green accent. A pristine, softly lit pharmaceutical manufacturing corridor recedes into shallow depth of field, gleaming stainless-steel cleanroom surfaces and frosted glass catching soft natural light from one side. In sharp foreground focus, a gloved hand in a crisp gown rests beside an open quality records binder and a tablet showing abstract data-trend lines, conveying meticulous inspection readiness and control. Calm, premium, authoritative atmosphere; no text, no logos, 4:3 aspect ratio.

faqs.q: How much notice does the FDA give a UK site before an inspection?
faqs.a: For foreign sites the FDA generally provides advance notice, often several weeks, to arrange access and visas, unlike the unannounced inspections common domestically in the US. However, you should never rely on lead time to fix systemic gaps. Treat your quality system as inspection-ready at all times so that notice is used for logistics and rehearsal, not remediation.

faqs.q: Is an FDA inspection the same as an MHRA or EU GMP inspection?
faqs.a: No. While the underlying GMP principles overlap heavily with EU GMP and ICH Q10, the FDA inspects against 21 CFR 210 and 211 and uses its own enforcement tools, including the Form FDA 483 and Warning Letters. Data integrity and the link between submission data and shop-floor reality are scrutinised especially closely. Map your existing systems against the US framework rather than assuming they are interchangeable.

faqs.q: What should we do if we receive a Form FDA 483?
faqs.a: Respond in writing, ordinarily within fifteen business days, addressing every observation individually. Each response should give a genuine root cause, specific corrective and preventive actions, realistic completion dates and an assessment of whether the issue affects other products or processes. A thorough, evidence-backed reply is the single best way to prevent a 483 escalating to a Warning Letter.
